name: umnet-napalm

metadata.name: umnet-napalm
metadata.summary: A custom version of NAPALM for UMnet

documentation:
# umnet-napalm
This is a project that augments the [NAPALM](https://napalm.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) library in ways that are relevant to our interests.
More specifically, new [getter functions](https://napalm.readthedocs.io/en/latest/support/index.html#getters-support-matrix) have been implemented to pull
data from routers and parse it into a vender agnostic format.

Currently, the following NAPALM classes have been augmented:
* IOS
* NXOS-SSH
* Junos
* PANOS (from [napalm-panos](https://github.com/napalm-automation-community/napalm-panos))

Have also written a NAPALM class for ASA - the community ASA napalm is based on the http API.

The following "getter" methods have been added:
* `get_ip_interfaces`
* `get_active_routes`
* `get_mpls_switching`
* `get_vni_information`
* `get_inventory`

functions.name: umnet_napalm.utils.abbr_interface
documentation:
Converts long version of interface name to short one
if applicable

functions.name: umnet_napalm.utils.age_to_datetime
documentation:
Across platforms age strings can be:
10y5w, 5w4d, 05d04h, 01:10:12, 3w4d 01:02:03,
50 days 10 hours (ASA)

functions.name: umnet_napalm.utils.resolve_nh
documentation:
Given a next hop IP and next hop table, search a list of
routes for that next hop and return LPMs that have a next hop
interface

functions.name: umnet_napalm.utils.resolve_all_nhs
documentation:
Goes through a list of routes and resolves the interface for any
next hops that don't already have them listed.

functions.name: umnet_napalm.utils.str_to_type
documentation:
Converts string output to obvious types.
empty quote is converted to "None".
ip_address, ip_interface, and ip_network are converted to their appropriate objecst
integers are converted to int

functions.name: umnet_napalm.utils.configure_logging
documentation: Configures logging for the module, or globally as indicated by the input

classes.name: umnet_napalm.utils.UMnetNapalmJsonEncoder
documentation:
custom json encoder that handles types
in our results that aren't encodable by default.
Reference the "Extending JSONEncoder" section of: https://docs.python.org/3/library/json.html

Currently ipaddress and timedelta types are the only non-encodable
types among the results

functions.name: umnet_napalm.panos.utils.system_state_value_to_type
documentation: Converts a string to an int, float, bool, date, or IP

functions.name: umnet_napalm.panos.utils.parse_system_state
documentation:
Parses "show system state", either filtered or unfiltered,
from a raw string into structured data.

Note that "show system state" output consists of a long list of top-level
entries that we're saving as a dictionary. Each entry can be a string (single or multi-line),
or a complex nested object of dicts, lists, and tuples.

classes.name: umnet_napalm.panos.utils.MutableTuple
documentation:
Class used to identify a tuple that's been parsed
from a string (dynamically created)

Some of these methods are expected to be inherited from
NAPALM. Extensions to NAPALM are defined in vendor-specific child classes

classes.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS._config_search
documentation: Does an xpath search of the effective running config and returns the results.

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS._get_effective_running_config
documentation: Gets effective running config and saves it to the local object

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS._get_system_state
documentation: runs 'show system state' and parses the returned pseudo-json into structured output

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS._get_mtu_and_comment
documentation:
Looks up mtu and comment on a particular interface or subinterface
object. Returns a dict with mtu and comment key/value pairs

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS._get_interface_mtus_and_comments
documentation:
Looks through the effective running config at network/interface for MTU configurations
and descriptions, aka "comments"
returns a dict keyed on interface names, with
dict vaules, eg results[interface_name] = {'mtu':mtu, and 'comment': comment }

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS._send_command
documentation: Send XML command to PANOS and get result

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS.get_inventory
documentation: Digs through "show chassis inventory" and "show system state" to pull out linecard and optics information

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS.get_ip_interfaces
documentation:
napalm_panos doesn't support "get_routing_instances".
While we could implement that function and combine it with "get_interfaces_ip" and
"get_interfaces", it's simpler to just run "show interface all" to get most of what we need.

Note that short of doing "show interface [x]" for every interface, I don't see a way
to get MTU, so for now it's getting set to 0.

methods.name: umnet_napalm.panos.panos.PANOS.get_active_routes
documentation: Gets active routes from all vsyses on the PAN
PANOS Parser

classes.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA

documentation: Netmiko open

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.close
documentation: Netmiko close

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.cli
documentation:
Executes an arbitrary list of commands and returns as a dict
mapping the executed command to its result.

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA._send
documentation: This is a copy of "_send_command" from the napalm ios driver

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA._send_command
documentation:
If the device is multi-context, sends the command
to all the contexts doing a 'changeto' in between each one.

If flatten_result is true it will return one long string
with '!!!! changeto {context} !!!!' added between each context's output.

Otherwise it will return the results as a dict of strings
keyed on the context names

If this is a non-context device, it will always return a string
with the main context data

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.multi_context
documentation:
Uses 'show version' output to determine if this is a multi-context
ASA or not

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.contexts
documentation: Returns a list of contexts

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.get_facts
documentation: Processes "show version" into napalm facts dict

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.get_ip_interfaces
documentation:
Does 'show interfaces' and extracts layer 3 output.
The 'description' field is the 'nameif' name.
For multi-context ASAs the context is stored as the vrf.

methods.name: umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.get_arp_table
umnet_napalm.asa.asa.ASA.get_active_routes
documentation: Parses "show route". The 'vrf' field is the context name

Netmiko-based napalm class for pulling data from an ASA.

Note this assumes that for multi-context devices, you are logging in as an
'admin' that has access to all the contexts.

classes.name: umnet_napalm.mock.MockParser

methods.name: umnet_napalm.mock.MockParser._send_command
documentation:
Retrieves command response from a file instead of a live device
File must be saved at the path provided to the mock constructor, named
based on the command with underscores instead of spaces.

Note that for Junos the "command" is actually the junos view

Overloads the _send_command methods
in the different parsers so that the data comes from
a file instead of a live device

methods.name: umnet_napalm.mock.MockJunos._send_command

You can pass a "path" attribute to the pyez View constructor
instead of a device and it will read the data from a file - woot.
Mock class for testing the Junos parser.

classes.name: umnet_napalm.ios.ios.IOS

methods.name: umnet_napalm.ios.ios.IOS._parse_label_value
documentation: Parses mpls label value into normalized data

methods.name: umnet_napalm.ios.ios.IOS._get_route_labels
documentation:
Runs "show bgp vpnv4 unicast labels" and parses the result.

The output is a dictionary with (vrf, prefix) as key
and the outbound label as a value, eg
output[ ("vrf_VOIP_NGFW", "0.0.0.0/0") ] = "12345"

methods.name: umnet_napalm.ios.ios.IOS.get_active_routes
documentation:
Parses "show ip route vrf *" for IOS. Will also run
"show bgp vpnv4 unicast labels" to get label bindings

If we run our nornir getters right we can pull the arp aging timer

methods.name: umnet_napalm.junos.junos.Junos._save_inventory_item
documentation:
Extracts data from a particular inventory item object.
Returns whether we care aobut this item or not (so we know whether or not)
to loop over its children

methods.name: umnet_napalm.junos.junos.Junos._get_inventory_part_number
documentation:
Extracts the part number from an inventory item
which, depending on the specific item, is stashed in
the part number, model number, or description field

methods.name: umnet_napalm.junos.junos.Junos.get_mpls_switching
documentation: Parses 'show route active-path table mpls.0'

methods.name: umnet_napalm.junos.junos.Junos._parse_mpls_label
documentation: Parses mpls-label parameter into a list of integers

methods.name: umnet_napalm.junos.junos.Junos._process_mpls_nh
documentation:
Parses 'nh interface', 'mpls_label', and nh_table values
into something our standardized outpur likes.
Returns a tuple (nh_interface:str, out_label:list, aggregate:bool )

methods.name: umnet_napalm.junos.junos.Junos.get_arp_table
documentation:
Pulls ARP table - napalm does have get_arp_table for junos but it doesn't
support VRFs :(

Because we have to dynamically query every VPN on the device we can't use
junos views for this since they're based on statically defined rpc calls.
Instead we'll have to do our own rpc calls under the hood.
Junos parser
